title: Ultimate Tanares Adventures - a casual and detailed unboxing
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0t_oTJZ6Irs

Daniel (@dungeondive) tackles a genuinely enormous unboxing: Ultimate Tanares Adventures from Jagged Ore Games, a review copy so big it fills an entire Kallax cube. It folds the remastered Arena: the Contest into a sprawling adventure campaign — 100+ quests, 100+ villains, a city phase, world maps and a choose-your-own-adventure narrative, plus both PvE and PvP rules. This is a game he's earmarked for his new in-person game group rather than solo, precisely because there's so much to manage. Highlights from the pile: three trays of characterful minis, a dedicated card box, and tiered loot decks climbing to named and legendary gear (the Blood Burner, dragon armor) that promise deep, ownable character builds. He's reassured to learn the comrade system covers under-four-player counts with simplified NPCs — no dreaded 'dummy hero' — and that the arena-style combat may keep the footprint small despite the epic scope. The Kingdom Death comparison recurs: a board-game action phase, and a separate city phase he suspects he'll love. He digresses happily about Elden Ring's DLC and the game's abundant nautical, undead-pirate quests. Overwhelming, high-quality, and clearly built for a full table of players.

----

Daniel is saving Tanares for his new game group because it's too much to manage solo. Which of your games are 'group-only' by sheer weight — and do you resent that, or enjoy having a reason to gather people?
